commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(tui): terminal escape sequence sanitization (#642)

## Summary

- Replace regex-based `strip_ansi` with a byte-level state machine in
`sanitize.rs` that catches all known terminal escape sequence families:
CSI, OSC (clipboard hijack, title change), DCS (key remapping), APC,
SOS, PM, 8-bit C1 controls (U+0080-U+009F), and dangerous C0 characters
(BEL, BS, DEL, null)
- Move sanitization from render time to ingestion time — every `update/`
handler that receives external data now calls `sanitize_for_display()`
before storing in `App` state, closing bypass paths where view code
rendered unsanitized data via `Span::raw`/`Span::styled`
- 39 targeted tests covering each escape family, attack vectors (OSC 52
clipboard injection, cursor repositioning, key remapping), edge cases
(unterminated sequences, nested/malformed, multibyte UTF-8), and clean
passthrough

## Files changed

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `tui/src/sanitize.rs` | Complete rewrite — state machine replaces
regex |
| `tui/src/markdown.rs` | Remove redundant `strip_ansi` call (input is
now pre-sanitized) |
| `tui/src/app.rs` | Sanitize agent/history data on initial connect |
| `tui/src/update/api.rs` | Sanitize agents, sessions, history, errors
at ingestion |
| `tui/src/update/streaming.rs` | Sanitize streaming text, thinking,
tool names, approvals |
| `tui/src/update/sse.rs` | Sanitize SSE agent reload, tool names,
distill stages |
| `tui/src/update/command.rs` | Sanitize recall results from memory API
|
| `tui/src/update/settings.rs` | Sanitize config JSON tree and save
errors |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo fmt -p aletheia-tui -- --check` clean
- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-tui` — 371 tests pass
- [x] `cargo check --workspace` clean

Co-authored-by: admin <admin@ardentleatherworks.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(get): resolve leased credentials from `fnox get` (#338)

## Summary
- Adds `produced_env_vars()` to the `LeaseBackend` trait so each backend
declares which env var keys it produces
- `fnox get` now checks lease backends first — if the requested key
matches a lease backend's output, it resolves the lease (with caching)
before falling back to provider secrets
- Extracts `resolve_lease` from `exec.rs` into `lease.rs` as a shared
function for both `fnox exec` and `fnox get`

## Test plan
- [ ] `fnox get AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` returns the leased credential (not
the master secret) when an AWS STS lease is configured
- [ ] `fnox get SOME_PROVIDER_SECRET` still works for non-lease keys
- [ ] `fnox exec` still resolves leases correctly (regression check)
- [ ] Cached leases are reused by `fnox get`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes touch credential leasing, caching/encryption, and environment
injection paths used by `fnox exec`/`get`, so regressions could affect
secret retrieval or lease reuse behaviour, though the scope is localized
and mostly refactoring/extension.
> 
> **Overview**
> `fnox get` now detects when the requested key is produced by a
configured lease backend and resolves that lease (reusing cached
credentials when possible) before falling back to normal secret/provider
resolution, including honoring `as_file` output.
> 
> Lease handling is refactored: the lease resolution/caching logic
previously embedded in `exec` is moved into `lease.rs` as a shared
`resolve_lease` that uses short-lived ledger locks (read → unlock →
async work → write) to reduce cross-process contention, and `fnox get`
injects only the backend/encryption-provider *consumed* secrets into the
environment when needed.
> 
> Backends now declare which env vars they may consume (and AWS STS
which it produces), and a new `FnoxError::LeaseContractViolation`
surfaces when a backend claims to produce a key but does not return it
at runtime; housekeeping updates include `hk.pkl` linter tweaks.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
f6736ad6eccfe4dae49b0518f531e6e6792d4774. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CIT: add pruning params to approximate conditional inference tree behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: App Store compliance audit and grading model fairness fixes

## Summary
Complete audit and fixes to prepare Pregrader for public App Store release.
Addresses Apple compliance requirements and grading model integrity.

## Changes

### 1. iOS Configuration (Info.plist)
- Removed unused NSMicrophoneUsageDescription (camera uses enableAudio: false)
- Fixed App Transport Security: removed NSAllowsArbitraryLoads blanket exception
- Added domain-specific exception for pregrader-production.up.railway.app only

### 2. Privacy Policy
- New PrivacyPolicyScreen with 7 sections: Data Collection, Image Handling,
  Third-Party Services, Logging, Analytics & Tracking, Data Retention, Your Rights
- Added GET /privacy-policy backend endpoint (required by App Store Connect)
- Added route and navigation link from home screen

### 3. Full 1-10 Grade Range
- Extended damage penalty thresholds: destroyed (+1.5), severe (+0.8),
  significant (+0.3), major surface (+1.0), cap 2.5
- Enhanced corners: expanded whitening score range (50%+ → 2.0-1.0)
- Surface analysis: major damage cap lowered to 3.0 (from 5.0)
- Fallbacks: lowered from 7.0 to 5.0 (and as low as 1.0 for extreme damage)

### 4. Real Confidence Scores
- Changed all confidence fallbacks from 0.8 to 0.5 (default "uncertain" state)
- Fallback count tracking: ≥3 failures → 0.3, ≥1 failure → 0.5
- Confidence propagates through combined_grading.py error handling

### 5. Fallback Grade Cleanup
- All analysis modules now return low confidence with fallback grades
- Failures don't silently return moderate scores anymore
- Added fallback flags for confidence calculation

### 6. Refuse-to-Grade UI
- Backend: grading_status logic based on confidence
  - refused: confidence < 0.4 (refuse to grade)
  - low_confidence: 0.4-0.6 (warn user)
  - success: confidence ≥ 0.6 (normal display)
- Frontend: new _buildRefusedScreen() and low-confidence warning banner

## Files Modified

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(mcp): add MCP server for secret-gated AI agent access (#343)

## Summary

- Adds `fnox mcp` command that starts an MCP server over stdio, acting
as a session-scoped secret broker for AI agents
- Two tools: `get_secret` (retrieve a secret by name) and `exec` (run a
command with secrets as env vars)
- Secrets are batch-resolved on first access (amortizes yubikey/SSO
cost), cached in memory, never persisted to disk
- Tools can be selectively enabled via `[mcp] tools` config in
`fnox.toml`

## Config

```toml
[mcp]
tools = ["get_secret", "exec"]  # default: both enabled
```

Claude Code integration (`.claude/settings.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "fnox": {
      "command": "fnox",
      "args": ["mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` compiles without errors
- [x] `cargo test` — all 118 tests pass
- [x] CLI ordering test passes (new `Mcp` variant is alphabetically
placed)
- [ ] Manual: pipe JSON-RPC `tools/list` to `fnox mcp` stdin → returns
enabled tools
- [ ] Manual: `tools/call` get_secret with an age-encrypted secret →
returns value
- [ ] Manual: `tools/call` exec with a command → output contains secrets
without exposing them in the MCP stream
- [ ] Manual: configure `tools = ["exec"]` only → `get_secret` not
listed, calls rejected
- [ ] Integration: configure in `.claude/settings.json` as MCP server

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Adds a new secrets- and command-execution server surface area (`fnox
mcp`) where bugs could leak secret material or allow unintended
subprocess behavior. Although opt-in and with some safeguards (tool
allowlist, non-interactive mode, output limits/timeouts), it touches
security-critical handling of secrets and process execution.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `fnox mcp` CLI subcommand that starts a Model Context
Protocol (MCP) server over stdio, intended for session-scoped secret
access by AI agents.
> 
> Introduces `src/mcp_server.rs` implementing two MCP tools—`get_secret`
(on-demand secret retrieval, rejecting `as_file=true`) and `exec` (run a
subprocess with `env=true` secrets injected, optional `as_file`
temp-file injection, bounded output capture, and configurable timeout).
Secrets are batch-resolved on first tool use and cached in-memory for
the session.
> 
> Extends config/schema to support `[mcp]` settings (`tools` allowlist
and `exec_timeout_secs`) with careful merge semantics to avoid overlays
unintentionally re-enabling tools; updates docs/CLI reference and adds
the `rmcp` dependency.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
4ead6769b38264973c891f4484076c6c4985dbb4. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commit.message:
fix(install.ps1): use ErrorActionPreference=Continue globally

Stop mode escalates all stderr from native commands (schtasks, icacls,
git, pip, winget) to terminating errors, causing false failures on
first install when schtasks tasks don't exist yet. Switch to Continue
globally and rely on explicit $LASTEXITCODE checks for real failures.

commit.message:
Fix card view not loading: forward-declare live vars in first script block

liveConnected and livePlayerName are declared in the second script
block but used by renderHoleCard() in the first. Even with var,
the second script block hasn't executed when the first block's
init calls renderHoleCard(), causing a ReferenceError. Fixed by
declaring them in the first script block so they exist at init time.
